task_updates.executor_notes: Pinpointed two connected discrepancies from the existing render and overlay formulas. First, for a large uncropped image at x=658/y=0 with size 2256x1692, OverlayEditor renders the full logical bounds while Remotion clips the DOM element to the 1920x1080 composition. At 0.4x preview scale, the overlay box is 902.4x676.8 at x=319.2/y=0, but the visible Remotion image is only 504.8x432 ending at x=824/y=432, so the overlay overhang is 397.6px right and 244.8px bottom. Second, for the cropped T2 case, the visible box geometry matches, but `object-fit: cover` still computes the source window from the full off-screen 2256x1692 box; the current render shows source x≈833..1460 instead of the fill-model x≈0..1410. Strategy A (viewport-intersection plus object-position compensation) is the only option that fixes both problems while staying render-only. Strategy B fixes the content centering issue but leaves the oversized-box geometry mismatch. Strategy C fixes the box overhang but not the hidden-source offset. Browser/devtools screenshot validation was still blocked by the sandbox, so this conclusion is based on the exact overlay/render formulas and the CSS geometry already established in T1/T2.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Root cause is identified and the fix strategy is chosen, but the evidence is formula- and source-based rather than live screenshots/devtools because sandboxed headless browser runs still produced no DOM/screenshot output. The chosen path is Strategy A: intersect render bounds with the composition and compensate with object-position, then apply the same display-only intersection in OverlayEditor.
